title: Browns surge 25 spots in ESPN ranking that bodes well for their future
description: The future of the Browns is very encouraging with this ESPN ranking.
fullText:
Browns surge 25 spots in ESPN ranking that bodes well for their future originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

The Cleveland Browns are not one of the top teams in the NFL right now. They're clearly one of the teams expected to finish around the bottom of the league in 2026.

But there aren't many teams that can compare to the optimism surrounding the future of the franchise. It's a strange thing to say about the Browns, considering recent history, but this Browns team is in a great spot as a franchise.

The reason why is simple: ESPN's Aaron Schatz, in his ranking of the teams with the best under-25 talent, placed the Browns fourth overall. A strong ranking for Cleveland, and that's without a blue-chip QB under 25 years old as well.

"4. Cleveland Browns. Last year's rank: 29. Blue chips: TE Harold Fannin Jr., OT Spencer Fano, DT Mason Graham, LB Carson Schwesinger," Schatz writes.

The Browns rank fourth in the NFL for talent under 25 years old, as they're only behind the New England Patriots, Houston Texans, and Chicago Bears.

All three of those teams are some of the most exciting teams in the NFL, and the only reason why the Browns aren't included in that grouping is the questionable quarterback position.

But, looking over the rest of the roster, it's clear the Browns' talent under 25 years old is comparable to the best teams around the league.

MORE: Seahawks trade idea lands Jackson powers-Johnson from Raiders for one draft pick

On defense, Carson Schwesinger, Mason Graham, Emmanuel McNeill-Warren, and Ronnie Hickman are some great young players to have confidence in.

On offense, Harold Fannin Jr., Spencer Fano, Quinshon Judkins, Dylan Sampson, KC Concepcion, and Denzel Boston are a fantastic group of young offensive players.

That list doesn't even include Shedeur Sanders, who's also under 25 years old. Overall, it's clear the Browns have some of the best young talent in the NFL.

While they might not be a top contender like some of the teams inside the top four of this ranking from Schatz, these under-25 players on the Browns roster are great and bode well for the team's future success.

It just got a lot harder for Cowboys to trade George Pickens

Bears urged by NFL exec to use Cole Kmet more amid top-10 TE ranking

If Bills trade Josh Allen, this would be the mind-blowing cost

49ers urged to trade Mac Jones if they receive an offer of at least a 2nd round pick

Rams urged to trade for Patriots’ DeMario Douglas to fix WR3 problem

title: Panthers Reacts Survey: What do we expect from Brady Christensen this year?
description: The Panthers offensive line is already in turmoil, and training camp has just begun.
fullText:
This was a question we wanted to ask before we learned of Taylor Moton’s recent medical situation. Christensen has long been both a versatile offensive lineman and a fan favorite. With Moton set to miss an unspecified amount of time this season, Christensen’s availability and progress are of even greater interest t0 the Panthers.

Christensen has played in 59 games in his five seasons with the Panthers, including 34 starts. Six of those starts have been at tackle (all in 2021) and 28 have been across both guard positions in the years since. It is unclear if Moton’s pending absence affected the Panthers decision to bring Christensen back beyond their interest in him as somebody who can back up all five positions along the offensive line.

There is also Christensen’s own recovery from injury to consider. He tore his Achilles tendon in Week 8 of the 2025 season. The typical timeline for returning from such an injury would have him practicing within as little as eight months and playing in games again as late as a year later. Many athletes also never return to their full pre-injury form after an Achilles tear.

Christensen was cleared for contact this summer before the Panthers signed him, so presumably he is well on the road to recovery. When and where that road will end, however, is a question that remains to be answered.

Now we turn the larger question of his future over to you, Panthers fans. What do you expect from Brady Christensen this year?

Welcome to SB Nation Reacts, a survey of fans across the NFL. Throughout the year we ask questions of the most plugged-in Panthers fans and fans across the country.

title: Rangers preparing move for Brighton & Hove Albion starlet Amario Cozier-Duberry
description: Rangers are preparing to formalise their interest in Brighton & Hove Albion winger Amario Cozier-Duberry. Derek McInnes and Fabian Hurzeler are at Ibrox and the AMEX Stadium respectively. The Scottish...
fullText:
Rangers are preparing to formalise their interest in Brighton & Hove Albion winger Amario Cozier-Duberry. Derek McInnes and Fabian Hurzeler are at Ibrox and the AMEX Stadium respectively. The Scottish Premiership giants aim to win a fiercely contested race for one of British football’s brightest young wide talents.

The Ibrox club have been closely tracking the 21-year-old, who can ‘create something out of nothing’, after his impressive rise since leaving Arsenal’s Hale End academy. However, Rangers are not alone in their pursuit. Belgian giants RSC Anderlecht recently tested Brighton’s resolve with a concrete £5 million bid, only for the Seagulls to firmly reject the opening offer.

That rejection has sparked an expansive multi-club transfer battle. Alongside Rangers, a host of European clubs are lining up for the explosive winger, with West Ham United, Hull City, Paris FC, and Red Bull Salzburg all monitoring his situation. The widespread interest underscores Cozier-Duberry’s high potential.

For Brighton, the priority remains protecting their investment. The Amex hierarchy are keen to persuade the former England youth international to commit his long-term future by signing an improved contract. Having signed him on a free transfer from Arsenal, Brighton view Cozier-Duberry as a core part of their long-term plans. However, his lack of guaranteed Premier League minutes has left the door open for rival clubs to offer tempting first-team opportunities.

Rangers see Cozier-Duberry as an ideal fit for their style of play. With European football on offer and the prospect of high-stakes domestic competition, the Ibrox club believe they can provide a compelling environment for the winger to take the next step in his development after an impressive 2025/26 loan campaign with Bolton Wanderers, where he racked up nine goals and 10 assists across all competitions.

With Anderlecht’s rejected bid setting a baseline valuation above £5 million, Rangers are expected to submit a formal offer in the coming days to test Brighton’s resolve.

title: Barcelona captain’s relationship with club deteriorates after serious World Cup injury – report
description: According to Catalunya Radio, relations between FC Barcelona and Frenkie de Jong have deteriorated significantly following the midfielder’s return from the FIFA World Cup with a severe knee injury. ...
fullText:
According to Catalunya Radio, relations between FC Barcelona and Frenkie de Jong have deteriorated significantly following the midfielder’s return from the FIFA World Cup with a severe knee injury.

Internal friction has mounted inside the Catalan club regarding how the physical setback occurred and how it was subsequently managed by the player and the Netherlands national team during the tournament.

The club officials are understood to be very angry and frustrated about the circumstances surrounding the player’s medical treatment while away on international duty.

Initial medical assessments conducted at the Ciutat Esportiva Joan Gamper indicate that the Dutch international will be sidelined for a period ranging between three to four months.

Out for 3-4 months. (Photo by Mike Hewitt/Getty Images)

While more tests are scheduled to take place in the coming days, early evaluations of De Jong’s right knee revealed significant instability and potential ligament damage, completely disrupting head coach Hansi Flick’s tactical planning ahead of the new season.

The club’s hierarchy believes the injury was severely exacerbated because the player pushed through intense discomfort to participate in the knockout rounds.

De Jong featured in pivotal matches while utilizing heavy bandaging and receiving painkilling injections, a medical approach that Barcelona’s staff feel compromised his long-term physical stability.

It is understandable that Barça are not at all happy with the developments, considering that De Jong already missed a chunk of matches last season due to injury and fitness issues.

De Jong’s injury has disrupted Flick’s plans. (Photo by Eric Alonso/Getty Images)

As per the report, a definitive decision regarding the exact clinical treatment pathway will be finalized on Friday, following a final round of specialised medical tests.

While the player’s camp has traditionally favoured conservative, non-invasive recovery methods to avoid the operating table, Barcelona’s institutional stance is clear.

The club is strongly pushing for immediate surgery to definitively resolve the underlying chronic instability in the knee joint and to ensure full healing and prevent subsequent relapses, even if it delays his competitive re-integration into the first-team squad until late autumn.

It remains to be seen what De Jong decides, with an official statement regarding his injury and recovery likely to arrive once the player chooses his next move.

title: Dayton basketball: Holmes 'ready to attack' second season on court in NBA
description: Jul. 22—KETTERING — As DaRon Holmes II signed autographs and posed for photos with Dayton Flyers fans at Lee's Famous Recipe Chicken, someone approached him and said, "Welcome back to Dayton. This is home, right?" "Just like you just said," Holmes said on Tuesday, July 21. "This is my other home.
fullText:
Jul. 22—KETTERING — As DaRon Holmes II signed autographs and posed for photos with Dayton Flyers fans at Lee's Famous Recipe Chicken, someone approached him and said, "Welcome back to Dayton. This is home, right?"

"Just like you just said," Holmes said on Tuesday, July 21. "This is my other home. It's always great to be back. I'm so comfortable here. The people here are always amazing with the hospitality. I'm just grateful I remember when it all first started."

Holmes arrived at the University of Dayton as a freshman five years ago. Now he's entering his third year in the NBA with the Denver Nuggets. This was his second appearance in as many years at Lee's, which he endorsed throughout his college career.

Holmes will spend a couple of days in Dayton, his visit overlapping with Obi Toppin's, Dayton's other recent All-American forward. Toppin will host a golf event, the Obi Open, on Wednesday, July 22, and then a sixth annual youth basketball camp on Thursday at the UD RecPlex.

Holmes hopes to return to Dayton during the 2026-27 season for a game at UD Arena. He hasn't had that opportunity since his final game with the Flyers in 2024.

"It's just hard because of our season," he said. "It's just very difficult. But I'm going to try my best."

The last time Holmes returned to Dayton, he had yet to play in an NBA game. He missed what would have been his rookie season after tearing his Achilles tendon in the NBA Summer League in 2024.

Holmes finally made his NBA debut last season. He appeared in 25 games, averaging 3.7 points and 1.4 rebounds in 8.4 minutes per game. He also appeared in 13 NBA G League games with the Grand Rapids Gold, averaging 20.9 points and 7.8 rebounds.

"It was fun," Holmes said. "I learned a lot after tearing my Achilles, just watching the games. It was weird battling two things at once. Am I going to be re-injured? And it's my first year in the NBA. It's like those two things were aligned, and it was very difficult to balance. But, overall, I gained great experience. I'm ready to attack this next year. I'm very excited."

Holmes did not play in the NBA Summer League this month but did practice with the Nuggets players on the Summer League roster in Las Vegas.

"It was a very valuable experience," he said. "I wish I could have played. It wasn't my decision, ultimately."

Holmes did play pickup basketball with NBA veterans in Las Vegas.

"I had a good showing," he said, "so that was fun. I'm just taking things one step at a time, doing anything I can."

Holmes reunited with Dayton head coach Anthony Grant and associate head coach James Kane in Vegas. They had dinner with former Flyers Toumani Camara and Nate Santos and former UD graduate assistant Leron Black, who's now an assistant coach with the Portland Trail Blazers, Camara's team.

Holmes watched the Flyers on TV often last season. He played a pickup game with them on campus during his visit to UD in 2025. He said he's ready for Dayton's latest big man with star potential, 7-foot-1 center Amaël L'Etang, to have a breakout season as a junior.

Holmes also knows one of the new Flyers well. He played at Montverde Academy in the 2020-21 season at the same time as 6-7 forward Jalen Haynes, a Cincinnati transfer who signed with Dayton in the spring. Holmes was a senior that season. Haynes spent a post-graduate year at Montverde after graduating from Dillard High School in Fort Lauderdale, Fla., in 2020.

"We used to scrimmage and practice a lot," Holmes said. "We have a good history. I'm glad he's going there. I think he's a very vital piece. He's going to be huge. He's physical. He's a good leader, too."

title: Dayton Dragons: Friend sacrifice fly caps comeback victory in 10th
description: Jul. 22—For almost five hours, the clock — metaphorically — threatened to strike midnight on the Dayton Dragons many times. They trailed 6-0, 7-3 and 13-10. But, at 11:59 p.m.
fullText:
Jul. 22—For almost five hours, the clock — metaphorically — threatened to strike midnight on the Dayton Dragons many times.

They trailed 6-0, 7-3 and 13-10.

But, at 11:59 p.m., the Dragons escaped midnight and defeat by putting the ball in play. Jacob Friend lofted a fly ball to deep left field, sending Julio Carreras home for the tie-breaking run and a 14-13 victory over Cedar Rapids with few left to witness it at Day Air Ballpark.

"It's always fun to be the guy," said Friend who was mobbed by teammates behind first base. "The goal there is just to pass it to the next guy. So that's what we were trying to do. I just got in the air enough."

Ninety-nine percent of the fans who were in the stadium for the first pitch cleared out after a one hour and 29-minute rain delay in the middle of the seventh inning. To be fair to those who left, the Dragons trailed 12-10. They had seen their money's worth for seven innings, but they sure missed a lot in the final three and a half innings.

The Dragons (49-40, 10-13 second half) faced a 13-10 deficit in the ninth. But Cedar Rapids reliever Nick Trabacchi did them a favor by walking Friend and Mason Neville to start the inning. After a pitching change and a strikeout, more good fortune came when Victor Acosta loaded the bases on a catcher's interference call.

Bases loaded, not a ball put in play, and the top of the order was next.

They rewarded the few handfuls of fans left. Peyton Stovall got his first hit with a two-run single to right, sending Acosta to third. Ryan McCrystal followed with a line drive to left for a sacrifice fly to score Acosta with the tying run.

The Kernels (37-52, 6-19) moved their free runner to third in the 10th on an infield single off the leg of pitcher Brady Afthim. The Kernels got tricky, but the Dragons were ready.

When Afthim struck out Graham Brown, Henry Kusiak did the steal-and-stop play to create a rundown in hopes the runner on third would score. Pinch runner Andy Lugo broke for home, but the Dragons let Kusiak go and retired Lugo in a 2-4-5-2-6 rundown to end the inning with the score tied.

"We kind of knew they we're going do the steal-stop there," Morillo said. "They like to do that a lot so we were ready. Even before the game we talked about it. And we executed it well, which is great."

The Dragons' 10th began with a strikeout. But Carerras stayed alert at second base, made some green-light eye contact with manager and third-base coach Julio Morillo, and stole third for one of the game's biggest plays.

"Huge stolen base by Julio in that situation," Morillo said. "He read it well. It was given to him. Why not? Maybe you don't do it with nobody out, but why not? You take a shot."

Tyson Lewis, who had three hits and an RBI, worked a great at-bat, fouling off five pitches before earning a 10-pitch walk. Then Friend ended it.

The Dragons also won because of the relief work of Afthim. In his first two appearances since joining the team he allowed three runs each time. This time he struck out three batters in the eighth on nine pitches for the rare immaculate inning.

"It was a lot of fun to watch," Friend said of his view from left field. "Me and the bullpen were screaming at each other running in after that. That might be the first time I've ever seen it happen in game. I loved it."

Afthim (2-0) allowed a run in the ninth, but he pitched three innings that finally held the Kernels in check.

After two innings, the game vibed like one that would require double-digit runs to win. Balls were being hit hard, others were finding holes and you just knew it would be a long night.

The Kernels brought the power early when Yasser Mercedes led off the game with a home run to deep left. Dragons starter David Lorduy couldn't keep the ball in the park in the second.

With two outs, Dameury Pena tripled and kept running to score on an error when Friend struggled to pick up the ball. Still, it was 2-0 with two outs. But a walk, homer by Graham Brown, walk, homer by 19-year-old Eduardo Tait (the Twins' No. 3 prospect) and the Kernels led 6-0.

The Dragons scored three in the second on four singles with the help of a balk and catcher's interference. Alfredo Alcantara got the first run home on a groundout. Acosta and McCrystal singled in the next two and it was 6-3.

Danny DeAndrade homered in the third for a 7-3 Kernels lead. Neville led off the fourth for the Dragons with his fifth home run, another run scored on Stovall's groundout and the score was 7-5. Friend hit a two-run double in the fifth to tie the score.

Lorduy lasted into the sixth and left with one on and one out. But the Kernels answered with Brown's two-run single in the sixth to lead 9-7. The single ricocheted off pitcher Graham Osman into shallow right field. If he had caught it or it had gotten through to an infielder, the inning would have ended with the score tied.

No worries. McCrystal and Lewis hit RBI singles to key a three-run sixth for a 10-9 lead.

And no worries for the Kernels. They got a run on a throwing error by third baseman Lewis and two more on a single by Miguel Briceno.

Then the rain was near. By the time the tarp was in place over the infield, the heavy rain began to fall and fans flooded the exits.

Eighty-nine minutes later the real fun began.

title: Chris Bosh Makes Case for LeBron James Heat Return
description: James and Bosh played together in Miami from 2010-14.
fullText:
Chris Bosh knows a thing or two when it comes to LeBron James and free agency.

In 2010, Bosh famously teamed up with James and Dwyane Wade in Miami to form the “Big Three,” which resulted in two NBA championships. Days before James appeared on ESPN to announce ‘The Decision’ in July 2010, Bosh and Wade posted a photo together at a table, with an empty chair indicating James would join them.

Now James is a free agent for the third time in his career and his former teammate is pushing for a reunion with the Heat. Bosh, who played seven seasons in Miami before retiring due to blood clots in 2019, said South Beach offers James a chance to win and work on his golf game simultaneously.

“When you’re older, when you’re ready to retire, you go to Miami,” Bosh joked in an interview with Front Office Sports.

Bosh has not been in touch with James regarding his current free agency, but thinks his friend and former teammate isn’t worried about the perception that will come with his next decision. The 41-year-old is reportedly considering the Cavaliers, Heat, Sixers, and Warriors. Multiple Western Conference executives previously told FOS they expect James to sign with either the Cavs or Warriors.

“I think legacy and reputation, that’s already built,” Bosh said to FOS. “I think now it’s just what he wants to do. You have to have so many things you have to consider. Family, your interests, I’m sure business interests as well and being competitive at the same time. I think some people can get too caught up in the legacy thing because they want their legacy to be his and that’s not how it is. He’s earned the right to make his own decision. No pun intended.

“So I think it will be really interesting. I think he does want to stay competitive but at the same time if he can have that work/life balance that would be the best situation for him and his family.”

As for when James will announce where he’ll be spending the 2026-27 season, that remains unclear. Speaking on his Game Over podcast earlier this week, the four-time MVP’s agent, Richa Paul, insisted that he and his star client are “not going to be rushed.”

The post Chris Bosh Makes Case for LeBron James Heat Return appeared first on Front Office Sports.

title: 2027 NFL Draft Summer Scouting Report: Kameryn Crawford, Edge, USC
description: The USC defense is making a turn up front and Kameryn Crawford is a big reason why.
fullText:
Summer is here, and it is everyone’s favorite time of the year, scouting time. Every year, we do Summer Scouting to preview college football for Bucs fans, but also give them an idea of names to follow and watch in the fall as needs pop up for Tampa Bay.

Are we going to write about quarterbacks? Offensive tackles? Edge rushers? You know it, but why? Because other NFL teams will draft them, just as they will draft every other position. It all matters whether these players end up on the Bucs, the Bears, or the Steelers. We write these so Bucs fans can be the most informed fans.

So if you are still with us, enjoy our latest Summer Scouting report as we prepare you for the Fall.

4-Star prospect according to 247 Sports

Under Armour All-American in high school

Lines up the most at ROLB (40% of snaps)

0 Passes batted at the line of scrimmage

Quickness and burst off the edge

Good length to get and win leverage

Shows some finesse in his pass rushing

Can generate power with a build-up

Crawford has some physical traits that are being relied upon in their raw form rather than being used in ways that could have led to more success. He is best suited to stand up on the edge rather than have his hand in the dirt to showcase his length and speed off the edge. Crawford has good awareness to provide run support as well, but I struggle to see him do that consistently. The quickness and burst make you want to be in on him as a prospect, but right now, it is hard to be all-in.

This article originally appeared on Bucs Wire: 2027 NFL Draft Summer Scouting Report: Kameryn Crawford, Edge, USC

title: Pogon Szczecin agree fee for Dorrington
description: Pogon Szczecin have agreed a deal for Tottenham defender Alfie Dorrington to move to Poland in permanent transfer.  The 21-year-old's move remains subject to personal terms but Pogon have permission to conduct a medical.  Dorrington spent last season on loan at Salford City in League Two, narrowly missing out on promotion through both the league proper and later the play-offs, having also had two spells at Scottish Premiership club Aberdeen.
fullText:
Pogon Szczecin have agreed a deal for Tottenham defender Alfie Dorrington to move to Poland in permanent transfer.

The 21-year-old's move remains subject to personal terms but Pogon have permission to conduct a medical.

Dorrington spent last season on loan at Salford City in League Two, narrowly missing out on promotion through both the league proper and later the play-offs, having also had two spells at Scottish Premiership club Aberdeen.

He has made a single appearance for Spurs since joining in 2018 and is an England under-19s international.

Pogon finished ninth in the Polish top-flight last season but typically harbour ambitions to compete for trophies and the European places at the top of the league.

title: Carson Beck first among top 4 QBs drafted to sign rookie deal
description: The top four quarterbacks selected in the 2026 NFL Draft were all unsigned as of Wednesday morning. Carson Beck officially ended the gridlock.
fullText:
The top four quarterbacks selected in the 2026 NFL Draft were all unsigned as of morning of Wednesday, July 22. Carson Beck ended the gridlock.

Beck signed his rookie deal, the Arizona Cardinals announced Wednesday. All seven members of the Cardinals’ 2026 draft class, who reported to training camp Wednesday, have now inked their rookie deals.

Beck was drafted in the third round (pick No. 65) in this year’s draft. The 24-year-old rookie is expected to battle with Jacoby Brissett and Gardner Minshew for Arizona’s starting quarterback job.

Beck played 55 college games with Miami (2025) and Georgia (2020-24). He passed for 11,725 yards and 88 touchdowns while compiling a 37-6 record as a starter.

According to Over The Cap, Beck’s four-year contract is worth $7.4 million.

Rookie contracts are slotted and predetermined. But the top four quarterbacks selected in the 2026 draft, Fernando Mendoza, Ty Simpson, Carson Beck and Drew Allar, were all unsigned as of Wednesday morning because of unresolved terms in their contracts.

Quarterbacks typically to have more leverage to negotiate certain details in their rookie contracts. Signing bonus cash flow, guaranteed money details and offset language are negotiable terms in rookie contracts.

Beck, Simpson and Allar are represented by agents part of the Creative Artists Agency (CAA) while Mendoza is represented by an agent in Excel Sports Management.

Now that Beck has signed his rookie deal, expect it to have a domino effect on the rest of the QB rookie class.

Follow USA TODAY Sports' Tyler Dragon on X @TheTylerDragon.

This article originally appeared on USA TODAY Sports: Carson Beck first among top 4 QBs drafted to sign rookie deal

title: Morgan Rogers aim sly dig at Arsenal after completing Chelsea move
description: Morgan Rogers has appeared to aim a subtle dig at Arsenal after completing his big-money move to Chelsea from Aston Villa, with the Gunners missing out on one of their major transfer targets.Arsenal h...
fullText:
Morgan Rogers has appeared to aim a subtle dig at Arsenal after completing his big-money move to Chelsea from Aston Villa, with the Gunners missing out on one of their major transfer targets.

Arsenal had reached an agreement with Rogers over personal terms and were confident they could win the race for his signature. However, once negotiations began with Aston Villa, it became clear that the Gunners were not prepared to meet the club’s demands for the attacker.

That situation allowed Chelsea to enter the race, and the Blues moved quickly to complete a deal for one of the most highly regarded players in England. Rogers has consistently delivered impressive numbers in goals and assists at club level and is expected to become an important player for Chelsea.

Arsenal have already moved on from the disappointment and are exploring other options as they look to complete the transfer window with a major signing. However, Rogers’ comments after joining Chelsea have attracted attention from supporters of both clubs.

Speaking after completing his transfer, Rogers praised Chelsea and highlighted his excitement about joining the club. According to Sports Illustrated, Rogers said:

“For me, Chelsea are the biggest club in London and a club I’ve always admired since I was a kid.

“I’m really excited about the project with the new manager, the players we’ve got and where the club is heading. That’s why I’m here and I can’t wait to get started.”

Rogers’ comments are similar to what many players say after joining a new club, and Arsenal are unlikely to be overly concerned by his words.

The Gunners will now focus on their remaining transfer plans and continue searching for other high-quality additions. Missing out on Rogers is a setback, but Arsenal remain determined to strengthen their squad before the window closes.

title: Official: Schalke 04 sign Maximilian Wöber from Leeds United
description: FC Schalke 04 have confirmed the signing of Maximilian Wöber on a free transfer.The 28-year-old defender joins the Royal Blues as a free agent after terminating his contract with Leeds United.“Maxi...
fullText:
FC Schalke 04 have confirmed the signing of Maximilian Wöber on a free transfer.

The 28-year-old defender joins the Royal Blues as a free agent after terminating his contract with Leeds United.

“Maximilian Wöber joins us with the experience of more than 170 top-flight games, and his time in Mönchengladbach and Bremen means he is already familiar with the Bundesliga," said Schalke 04 director of professional football Youri Mulder.

“We are convinced that he will be able to rediscover his best form with us. Our coaching staff, together with Miron Muslic, will work closely with him. As a player, his attributes give us the flexibility to use him in a number of different defensive set-ups.”

Wöber arrives after an injury-hit 2025/26 campaign on loan at Werder Bremen and is looking forward to the next chapter of his career.

“Schalke 04 proved a very exciting opportunity for me right from the start. After some really positive talks, I’m looking forward to taking on a new challenge at a very special club, who were deservedly promoted. Playing in the Bundesliga for Schalke poses an exciting challenge. I would now like to settle in as quickly as possible and get to know my new teammates.”

title: Game #102, Athletics vs. Diamondbacks Game Thread
description: The series win is on the line today in Phoenix
fullText:
The A’s will make a second stab at a series win this afternoon at Chase Field in Phoenix against the Arizona Diamondbacks. After taking the first game of the series 5-2 and then dropping last night’s matchup 6-5 in ten innings, the A’s will go for the win before heading to Minnesota to finish the road trip against the Twins.

Starting today for the Kelly-Green and Gold will be Gage Jump. Jump, the 23-year-old lefty is currently 3-5 with a 3.78 ERA over ten starts in 2026. He’ll go up against 37-year-old righty Merrill Kelly for the D-backs. Kelly is 8-7 with a 5.20 ERA over t17 starts this season. He’s struck out sixty-two opponents in 98.2 innings.

title: Arizona Digest. Cardinals sign rookie QB Carson Beck
description: A roundup of Arizona sports news and events during the summer of 2026.
fullText:
Summer is here in Arizona, and there’s plenty of activity, including college sports and a new season for the Phoenix Mercury, Phoenix Rising FC and Arizona Rattlers. Here are updates on news and events happening around Arizona sports:

Be sure to 

The Arizona Cardinals have come to contract terms with all seven of their 2026 draft picks after agreeing on a four-year deal with their last remaining unsigned rookie, third-round quarterback Carson Beck.

Beck, the former standout at Georgia and the University of Miami, is expected to get the chance to start some games this season for the Cardinals despite the presence of veteran QBs Jacoby Brissett and Gardner Minshew II.

Beck and the Cardinals were scheduled to report to training camp at State Farm Stadium on Wednesday, July 22.

The team also announced it has signed defensive lineman Coziah Izzard. Izzard spent his rookie season in 2025 on the practice squads of the Saints and Chiefs after entering the league with Kansas City as an undrafted free agent from Penn State.

Beck was a member of two national championship teams with the Bulldogs as a backup and, after transferring to Miami, helped lead the Hurricanes to the national title game last season. Miami went 13-3 last season with Beck completing 72.4 percent of his passes for 3,813 yards, 30 touchdowns and 12 interceptions.

This article originally appeared on Arizona Republic: Arizona Digest. Cardinals sign rookie QB Carson Beck

title: Jeff Bezos Reportedly Might Join Bid for Liverpool Stake
description: A deal could value the Premier League club at roughly $6 billion.
fullText:
A group led by the son-in-law of Indian steel magnate Lakshmi Mittal is in talks to buy a minority stake in Premier League soccer team Liverpool from Fenway Sports Group—and Amazon founder Jeff Bezos reportedly may join the bid.

FSG, which in addition to Liverpool owns the Red Sox and backs the PGA Tour’s for-profit commercial entity, confirmed on Tuesday there are discussions with a group led by Amit Bhatia, son-in-law of Lakshmi Mittal, about a “strategic minority investment” in Liverpool. The Mittal family, which includes Aditya Mittal—a Celtics minority owner who just lost out on buying the Seahawks to a group led by 49ers minority owner Vinod Khosla—is also part of the group (Aditya is Lakshmi’s son).

A deal could value Liverpool at around $6 billion, the Financial Times reported.

After that news came out, Sky News reported that Bezos has “held discussions” about joining the consortium, although the report cautioned there is no guarantee he will end up investing. Representatives for two of Bezos’s companies, Amazon and Blue Origin, did not respond to requests for comment (Bezos also owns the Washington Post).

Although he has yet to wade into the world of sports ownership, Bezos’s name often comes up when teams are for sale given his deep pockets (he is the world’s fourth-richest person with a net worth of almost $254 billion, according to Forbes). As examples, he was linked to the Commanders in 2021, before they ultimately sold to Josh Harris for $6.05 billion, and his name came up when the Celtics were for sale (although a source close to Bezos later denied his interest).

Bhatia, who for the last 18 seasons has co-owned the EFL Championship’s Queens Park Rangers, stepped down from the team’s board on Tuesday and agreed to “transfer” his stake in the club to majority owner Ruben Gnanalingam. The statement announcing his departure did not mention the potential Liverpool bid.

A source familiar with the matter tells Front Office Sports that discussions with the Bhatia-led group are preliminary and there is no guarantee a deal will be reached. If a deal does happen, it would not be the first time FSG has sold a minority stake in Liverpool; in 2023, private-equity firm Dynasty Equity acquired a minority stake in the soccer club.

Harsh Talikoti of investment bank Houlihan Lokey tells FOS that the reported $6 billion valuation “underlines how far premium sports franchises have moved from trophy assets to institutional-grade investments.”

“Scarcity is the defining feature,” Talikoti said. “Clubs like Liverpool, with more than 130 years of history, 20 English league titles, six European Cups and a genuinely global fanbase, simply cannot be replicated, and their diversified revenues across media rights, matchday and commercial partnerships make them attractive to long-term capital.”

That the Bhatia group is eyeing a minority stake also underscores a growing trend in pro sports. Vinod Khosla, who just bought the Seahawks, owns a minority stake in the 49ers that he will have to divest before his deal for Seattle can be completed. David Tepper, who in 2018 bought the Panthers, was previously a minority investor in the Steelers. Leagues like the NFL generally view potential majority owners more favorably if they are first minority owners, because those minority deals require due diligence that makes it easier for people to be vetted once they’re trying to become majority owners, multiple sources have told FOS.

Another reason the Bhatia-led group may be appealing to the Premier League is that the Mittal family has been building a portfolio of sports investments that shows they can be good stewards of franchises. In addition to Aditya Mittal’s $1 billion minority investment in the Celtics, the family’s patriarch, Lakshmi Mittal, purchased the Rajasthan Royals of the Indian Premier League in May for $1.65 billion.

Talikoti said it’s “telling” that the interest in Liverpool comes from a “family with an established sports portfolio,” which represents a “sign of capital converging across leagues on the same underlying thesis.”

“Minority stakes have become the preferred entry point, letting investors gain exposure to that growth story while incumbent owners retain control and crystallize value,” Talikoti added.

Editors’ note: RedBird Capital Partners owns a stake in Fenway Sports Group. RedBird IMI, in which RedBird Capital Partners is a joint venture partner, is the primary investor in Front Office Sports.

The post Jeff Bezos Reportedly Might Join Bid for Liverpool Stake appeared first on Front Office Sports.

title: Emmanuel Acho issues apology to Deebo Samuel for harsh words
description: After calling Samuel a "glorified mascot" Emmanuel Acho issues an apology.
fullText:
It was inevitable Emmanuel Acho would eventually issue some sort of response after being called out by Deebo Samuel on X following Acho’s comments towards the one-time NFL All-Pro.

During a rant on the Speakeasy talk show, Acho would unleash an array of harsh words about current NFL free agent Samuel, explaining why a reunion in the Bay Area would not benefit the 49ers.

“I’m not gonna pay a glorified mascot top-tier money…If you’re a hype man, I’m not paying you to be a hype man. I’m paying you to get 1,400 yards.” Acho told Speakeasy co-host, LeSean McCoy.

MORE: Deebo Samuel calls out Emmanuel Acho for “glorified mascot” comments

On the same day, Samuel would take to his X account, issuing a response to Acho for calling him a mascot and telling the one-time player turned podcast host, “who gave him a voice, you play 3 years gang, 3 different teams.”

A harsh, but true dig at the former NFL linebacker, as he was only in the league from 2012 to 2014 with the Philadelphia Eagles.

The message seemed to get across, though, as Acho immediately took to his show the following afternoon to apologize to Deebo Samuel.

“Deebo, that’s on me. I apologize. That’s on me. Every now and then, when you give a lot of takes, you’re gonna give a take that you don’t even realize crosses the line. It was just mean. I don’t like being mean.” said the Speakeasy co-host.

Whether or not Samuel accepts the apology remains to be seen, but it was still a decent gesture from Acho, who clearly went overboard with the comments in the heat of conversation.

title: Cody Rhodes Didn’t Want to Be in John Cena’s Iconic Heel Turn?
description: Cody Rhodes has revealed that he was far from certain heading into one of WWE‘s most memorable moments of 2025, […]
fullText:
Cody Rhodes has revealed that he was far from certain heading into one of WWE‘s most memorable moments of 2025, telling Good Hang With Amy Poehler that he was “trepidatious” about his involvement in John Cena‘s heel turn at Elimination Chamber and that his overriding concern was not ruining the moment for a performer who had never done anything like it before.

The Elimination Chamber moment saw Cena turn heel for the first time in 22 years, with Rhodes becoming the victim of a three-on-one assault at the hands of Cena, The Rock, and Travis Scott. The angle set up their feud heading into WrestleMania 41, where Cena competed in his final WrestleMania match. Rhodes described the very different emotional states he and Cena were in going into the moment.

“With John, if you’ve ever seen it, the moment where he hugs me and his face changes, he was so excited,” Rhodes said. “I was more trepidatious in terms of he had never done that and it didn’t even make sense to do it at this point. He had never gone rogue, gone bad. So I just didn’t want to screw that massive moment up.”

Rhodes then opened up about the friendship that underpins that professional trust, tracing it back to his early years in WWE when he used to drive Cena on the road.

“He taught me everything I know because I drove him around for two years before he got his big tour bus,” Rhodes said. “John was such a fun riding partner. A lot of strict rules. He’s not gonna agree with this, but I was a ‘job guy’, I was laying on my back looking at the lights every night. I can’t think of another guy who didn’t need to take the time. He definitely didn’t need to, he was the busiest guy on the roster. He took the time.”

title: Florida LB Myles Graham talks up freshman he's most excited about
description: Myles Graham has been impressed by one Gators freshman in particular.
fullText:
The press has often asked players and coaches during SEC Media Days about freshmen who have made an impact this spring and summer. While most interviewees have elected to prop up their entire freshman class, Florida linebacker Myles Graham did single out a player who he thought has stood out in his mind.

"Absolutely. On defense, I would say incoming freshman would probably be KJ Ford. He's just a natural kind of pass rusher. He's just a natural football player. He's just very limber, loose. He's very mobile. He's fast," Graham said. "I think he's going to be a great player for us this year and the next years to come."

Ford committed to the Gators over the likes of Alabama, Ohio State, Texas A&M and USC, so he enters Florida with a lot of hype and expectations.

As for Graham, he is entering his junior season in Gainesville and will look to be a leader for Brad White and the Florida defense.
